name: BPC-157
description: A 15-amino-acid synthetic peptide that is among the most extensively studied repair-focused compounds in preclinical science. Rodent studies have investigated its role in stimulating new blood vessel formation (angiogenesis), accelerating wound tissue closure, and protecting the stomach and intestinal lining from experimentally induced damage. It has also been studied for tendon and ligament tissue response and its interactions with growth factors such as VEGF. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 99.4%.
shortDesc: A 15-amino-acid synthetic peptide that is among the most extensively studied repair-focused compounds in preclinical science. Rodent studies have investigated its role in stimulating new blood vessel formation (angiogenesis), accelerating wound tissue closure, and protecting the stomach and intestinal lining from experimentally induced damage.

faq.q: What purity is Velox Peptides BPC-157?
faq.a: BPC-157 is supplied at 99.4% HPLC-verified purity. A certificate of analysis is provided with every order. Every batch is third-party tested before dispatch.

faq.q: How is BPC-157 supplied and reconstituted for research use?
faq.a: BPC-157 is supplied as a lyophilised powder in sterile single-use vials. For in vitro research use, reconstitution is performed with bacteriostatic water. See the reconstitution calculator for volume calculations based on required concentration.

faq.q: What is BPC-157 used for in preclinical research?
faq.a: BPC-157 is studied in the peer-reviewed literature across the pathways described in the research overview above. Velox Peptides supplies BPC-157 strictly as a research reagent for in vitro use by qualified researchers. It is not a medicinal product and is not for human or veterinary consumption.

faq.q: What is the CAS number for BPC-157?
faq.a: The CAS registry number for BPC-157 is 137525-51-0. This identifier is listed on every batch certificate of analysis supplied with orders.

name: TB-500
description: A synthetic version of Thymosin Beta-4, a protein naturally present in mammalian tissue. Preclinical studies investigate its role in regulating actin — a structural protein that controls how cells move and organise during tissue repair. Animal studies have examined its effects on connective tissue response, cellular migration to injury sites, and cardiac tissue following experimentally induced damage. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 98.8%.
shortDesc: A synthetic version of Thymosin Beta-4, a protein naturally present in mammalian tissue. Preclinical studies investigate its role in regulating actin — a structural protein that controls how cells move and organise during tissue repair.

name: BPC-157 & TB-500 Mix
description: A single lyophilised vial combining 10mg BPC-157 and 10mg TB-500 — the two most widely studied tissue-repair peptides in preclinical science. BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound-157) is a 15-amino-acid synthetic peptide investigated in rodent models for roles in angiogenesis, wound tissue closure, and mucosal protection. TB-500 (synthetic thymosin beta-4 fragment) is studied for its actin-regulating properties and effects on cellular migration in tissue-response models. Combining both compounds in a single vial simplifies reconstitution and research protocols where both are used alongside each other. Supplied as a single 20mg co-lyophilised vial (10mg of each component). HPLC purity 98.5%.
shortDesc: 10mg BPC-157 and 10mg TB-500 combined in a single lyophilised vial. A convenient blend for tissue-repair research protocols where both compounds are studied in combination — one reconstitution, two compounds.

faq.q: What is in the BPC-157 & TB-500 Mix vial?
faq.a: Each vial contains 10mg of BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound-157) and 10mg of TB-500 (synthetic thymosin beta-4 fragment), co-lyophilised in a single sterile vial — 20mg total peptide content.

faq.q: What purity is the BPC-157 & TB-500 Mix?
faq.a: The BPC-157 & TB-500 Mix is supplied at 98.5% HPLC-verified purity for both components. A batch-specific certificate of analysis documenting purity, mass spectrometry confirmation, and appearance is provided with every order.

faq.q: How is the BPC-157 & TB-500 Mix reconstituted for research use?
faq.a: The mix is supplied as a co-lyophilised powder in a single sterile vial. For in vitro research use, reconstitution is performed with bacteriostatic water. See the reconstitution calculator for volume calculations based on your required concentration.

faq.q: What is the difference between the BPC-157 & TB-500 Mix and the BPC-157 + TB-500 Bundle?
faq.a: The Mix supplies both compounds co-lyophilised together in a single 20mg vial — one reconstitution step for both. The Bundle supplies BPC-157 and TB-500 as two separate individual vials (10mg each), allowing independent dosing and reconstitution of each compound. The Mix suits protocols where both compounds are always used together; the Bundle offers greater flexibility.

name: CJC-1295 wo DAC
fullName: Growth Hormone Releasing Factor Analogue (no DAC)
description: A synthetic analogue of the natural signal the brain uses to trigger growth hormone release. The absence of a Drug Affinity Complex (DAC) means it produces a shorter, sharper pulse of GH release — more closely mimicking the natural physiological pattern than longer-acting variants. Animal studies have measured its effects on GH and IGF-1 concentrations. Frequently studied alongside Ipamorelin in GH secretion research. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 99.1%.
shortDesc: A synthetic analogue of the natural signal the brain uses to trigger growth hormone release. The absence of a Drug Affinity Complex (DAC) means it produces a shorter, sharper pulse of GH release — more closely mimicking the natural physiological pattern than longer-acting variants.

name: Tesamorelin
description: A synthetic analogue of the full-length growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH), with structural modifications that slow its breakdown and extend its active window. Investigated in preclinical and clinical research for its effects on GH and IGF-1 production, and studied in specific patient groups for its influence on visceral fat distribution. One of the most clinically researched GHRH analogues available. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 98.6%.
shortDesc: A synthetic analogue of the full-length growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH), with structural modifications that slow its breakdown and extend its active window. name: DSIP
description: A 9-amino-acid neuropeptide first isolated from rabbit blood during deep sleep states. Animal studies investigate its effects on the architecture of sleep — particularly slow-wave and delta sleep stages — as well as its interaction with the body's stress-response axis. Researchers study it as a naturally occurring signal involved in sleep regulation and neuroendocrine balance. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 98.7%.
shortDesc: A 9-amino-acid neuropeptide first isolated from rabbit blood during deep sleep states. Animal studies investigate its effects on the architecture of sleep — particularly slow-wave and delta sleep stages — as well as its interaction with the body's stress-response axis.

name: Selank
description: A synthetic 7-amino-acid peptide based on a fragment of the immune protein tuftsin. Rodent studies have documented calmer behaviour in treated animals without observed sedation — a profile that distinguishes it from classical anxiolytic compounds in research settings. Studies have also measured increased BDNF (a protein involved in brain cell survival and connectivity) in neuronal tissue, alongside effects on GABAergic and serotonergic signalling pathways. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 98.9%.
shortDesc: A synthetic 7-amino-acid peptide based on a fragment of the immune protein tuftsin. Rodent studies have documented calmer behaviour in treated animals without observed sedation — a profile that distinguishes it from classical anxiolytic compounds in research settings.

name: Semax
description: A synthetic 7-amino-acid peptide based on a section of the stress hormone ACTH, with a stabilising tail added to improve its reach within biological systems. Preclinical studies have measured increased BDNF expression — a protein associated with brain cell growth and survival — in neuronal tissue following administration. Neuroprotective effects have also been studied in animal models of experimentally induced brain ischaemia. Widely researched in Eastern European neuroscience institutions. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 99.0%.
shortDesc: A synthetic 7-amino-acid peptide based on a section of the stress hormone ACTH, with a stabilising tail added to improve its reach within biological systems. name: Mots-C
description: A 16-amino-acid peptide with an unusual origin — its genetic instructions come from the DNA inside mitochondria (the cell's energy-producing structures), rather than from the cell nucleus. Under metabolic stress conditions, it travels to the nucleus and activates AMPK — an enzyme that acts as a cellular energy sensor and regulator. Animal studies have examined its influence on glucose metabolism and metabolic gene expression, making it a subject of interest in mitochondrial and metabolic pathway research. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 98.5%.
shortDesc: A 16-amino-acid peptide with an unusual origin — its genetic instructions come from the DNA inside mitochondria (the cell's energy-producing structures), rather than from the cell nucleus. name: NAD+
description: A coenzyme present in every living cell and essential for mitochondrial energy production and sirtuin enzyme activity. Sirtuins are proteins involved in DNA repair, gene regulation, and cellular stress responses. Research documents measurable declines in NAD+ levels in aged animal models, and studies examine whether restoring NAD+ influences DNA repair activity and mitochondrial function. A foundational research reagent for cellular metabolism and ageing pathway studies. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 99.5%.
shortDesc: A coenzyme present in every living cell and essential for mitochondrial energy production and sirtuin enzyme activity. Sirtuins are proteins involved in DNA repair, gene regulation, and cellular stress responses.

name: Retatrutide

sizes.note: Pre-loaded pen format — no reconstitution required.

description: A synthetic peptide that simultaneously activates three distinct metabolic receptors — GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon — each involved in regulating energy balance, insulin response, and fat metabolism. By engaging all three pathways at once, it offers researchers a tool for studying multi-receptor metabolic signalling in ways that single-agonist compounds cannot. Among the most actively investigated next-generation metabolic research peptides. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 98.8%.

name: GHK-Cu
description: A naturally occurring tripeptide found in mammalian blood that carries a copper atom. In skin cell and animal research models, it has been studied for its ability to upregulate the genes responsible for producing collagen and elastin — the structural proteins that give tissue its strength and elasticity. Studies have also examined its antioxidant activity and its role in regulating enzymes involved in tissue remodelling. One of the most researched copper-peptide compounds in dermatological science. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 99.0%.

name: Glutathione
description: A naturally occurring tripeptide antioxidant present in virtually every mammalian cell. It plays a central role in protecting cells from oxidative damage by neutralising free radicals, and is essential for mitochondrial function and cellular redox balance. Research in aged animal models documents measurable glutathione decline, and studies examine whether restoring levels influences DNA repair activity and oxidative stress markers. A widely used research reagent in ageing, mitochondrial, and cellular protection studies. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 99.2%.
shortDesc: A naturally occurring tripeptide antioxidant present in virtually every mammalian cell. It plays a central role in protecting cells from oxidative damage by neutralising free radicals, and is essential for mitochondrial function and cellular redox balance.

name: Melanotan II
description: A synthetic analogue of alpha-MSH that binds to multiple melanocortin receptors (MC1R, MC3R, MC4R, MC5R) simultaneously. These receptors are involved in pigmentation, energy regulation, and neurological signalling. Animal studies have examined its effects on melanin production pathways and on receptor-mediated signalling across several tissue systems. Its ability to interact with multiple receptor subtypes makes it a useful tool for multi-pathway receptor research. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 99.3%.
shortDesc: A synthetic analogue of alpha-MSH that binds to multiple melanocortin receptors (MC1R, MC3R, MC4R, MC5R) simultaneously. These receptors are involved in pigmentation, energy regulation, and neurological signalling.

name: Dihexa
description: A synthetic peptide derived from an angiotensin IV analogue, engineered to resist enzymatic breakdown for greater stability in biological environments. Preclinical research investigates its interaction with the HGF/c-Met signalling axis — a pathway associated with the formation and strengthening of synaptic connections between brain cells. Rodent experiments have examined dendritic spine density (a structural measure of synaptic connectivity) and spatial memory task performance in animal models. Among the newer compounds in cognitive neuroscience peptide research. For in vitro research use only. HPLC purity 98.5%.
shortDesc: A synthetic peptide derived from an angiotensin IV analogue, engineered to resist enzymatic breakdown for greater stability in biological environments.
